refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly focus on the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, clarifying the performance requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and validate p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator within latency budget.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency communication protocols optimized for teleoperation.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes near the worksite to reduce network hops.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation data traffic over other network traffic.
DP4: Employ real-time telemetry compression and efficient encoding.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Implement edge computing nodes near the worksite to reduce network hops.

potential_sources: Dependency on network infrastructure and vehicle communication modules.
Interaction with safety services for latency monitoring.
coupling_check.notes: Edge computing reduces latency coupling by localizing processing, minimizing cross-module dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of edge computing infrastructure at the worksite.
Risk of increased system complexity and maintenance overhead.
Risk that network conditions may still degrade latency beyond target.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating telemetry compression (DP4) if latency targets are not met after edge computing deployment.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realization of teleoperation communication, latency management, and operator interface.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for communication between Teleoperation module and Vehicle module over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time telemetry data sent from Vehicle to Teleoperation for operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control tasks received from NGHE Solution or Site Manager.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency management logic and communication handling; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle handles local control but latency logic is less contained here, higher diffusion risk.
Safety Services monitors but does not implement latency control; higher diffusion risk.
System-level coordination but not direct latency control; moderate diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control and teleoperation communication logic are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and simplify ownership.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for network interface may cause cross-module latency issues.
If Teleoperation module grows too complex, risk of diffusion into NGHE Solution or Safety Services.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module complexity and consider refactoring if diffusion risk increases; clarify interface contracts with Vehicle.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (edge computing) directly addresses the latency FR with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some latency-related logic may diffuse into Vehicle module due to network interface dependencies.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment of latency logic, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry and network monitoring tools at the Teleoperation module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define strict interface contracts between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to limit diffusion.
Plan for telemetry compression integration if latency targets are not met.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and DP integration (e.g., telemetry compression) is needed to fully mitigate latency risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator within latency budget.

revise.why: To incorporate additional latency reduction techniques such as telemetry compression and traffic prioritization.

assumptions: Edge computing infrastructure is available and reliable at the worksite.
Network conditions conform to agreed parameters.
open_risks: Network degradation beyond assumptions impacting latency.
Complexity growth in Teleoperation module leading to diffusion.
evidence_needed: Interface performance metrics between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
